content.full_text:
California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
september 11, 1990
Debra L. cauble
sr. Deputy city Attorney
city of san Jose
151 west Mi,ssion Street
San Jose, CA 9 511-0
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. I-9o-427
Dear Ms. cauble:
You have requested advice on behalf of San Jose Planning
conmissioner salvatore caruso concerning his duties under the
conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the
trActtr) pursuant to ne'gulation 18329(c) (coPy enctosed).1 we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to
negutation fefzs (c1 because we do not have enough facts about each
ooiernmental decision involved herein to advise hirn with
6erta inty . 2
OUESTIONS
1. May Planning conmissioner caruso represent applicants in
Land use priceedings before the city planning director, or discuss
these matLers with city planning department staff on behalf of
appl icants?
2. 
Does the answer to Question No. 1 depend on whether a
decj.sion made by the city planning director is appealable to the
planninq commislion or to the city council?
1 cor"arrr"nt code Sections 81000-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. commission
iegutations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations. Section
ie6oo, et EeS-- A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
oivision o ot the california Code of Regulations'
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
irnnunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice'
(sectioir a:r:.a; negulation 18329(c) (3), copy enclosed')
428 J Street, Suite 8O0 . P.o. Box 8O? . sacramento cA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322'5660


Our Fi■e No. I-90-427
Page 2
3. May enployees of Planning conrnissioner Caruso's
architectural consul-ting business represent applicants in Land use
proceedings before the city planning director or before city
planning department staff?
CONCLUSTON
1. The Act prohibits a public official 
who has a material
financial interest in a decision before his agency fron attenpting
to use his official 
position to inftuence that decision.
Therefore, Cornmissioner caruso mav not aDDear before hj,s own
agency (the planning commissi,on) or contact department of city
planning staff (including the director of planning) to represent a
client regarding a matter which is, or may corne, before his own
agency. Similarly, Commissioner Caruso cannot represent a client
before the planning director or department of city planning staff
when the decision to be made is one which is appealable to, and
nay be reconsidered by, the planning commj.ssion. The prohibition
applies because, in both these situations, the decision to be made
is ttwithin or beforerr the public official's 
own agency.
2. Generally speaking, a public official 
can represent the
interests of a client before a city agency not under the budgetary
or appointive control of the official's 
own agency. This rneans
that Coumissioner caruso mav aDDear before the director of
planning or his or her staff to represent a client as lons as the
tollowing two conditions are met: (1) the decision is one which
is appealable directly to the city council and, (2) Commissioner
carulo does not purport to act on behalf of the planning
conmission.
3. The Act does not prohibit an emPloyee of conmissioner
caruso's architectural consulting business from representing
applicants before the director or staff of the departnent.of city
pllnning, before the planning commission, or before the city
counciL. Holrever, Conmissioner caruso is disqualified frorn
participating in decisions before the planning comrnission which
witt frave a reasonably foreseeable and material financial effect
on his architectural ionsulting business, or on a source of income
to hiro of $25o or more, or on any other financial interest of the
conmissioner.
FACTS
Commissioner caruso is a design professional who serves on
the city's planning conrnission. He also is the sole os/ner of an
architecturaL consulting business.
In addition to being head of the dePartment of city planning,
the director of planning has multiple functions, powers, and
duties which are enumerated in the city's code. (san Jose
Municipal code s 2.2L.97O.) one of the primary . responsj,bilities
oi ifr"'planning director is to assist the planning commission in
itr" -p..i".ranc; 
of its functions, powers, and duties' 
Another is


Our F■■e No. I-90-427
Page 3
to give information and advice to the city council as may be
needed concerning planning and development matters. Another is to
serve as hearing officer and decisionmaker in various land use
proceedings, such as site development pernit applications and
tentative subdivision naps. Most of the planning director's
decisions are appealable directly to the ptanning comruission.
Hovrever, in sone situations, such as the issuance or denial of
sidewalk cafe perrnits, his decisions are appealable directly to
the city council.
The planning conrnission has no budgetary or appointive
control over the director of planning or his or her departnent,
the department of city planning.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits a public official 
from naking,
participating in naking or in any way attenpting to- inf I'uence a
governrn-ntal decision in which the official 
has a financial
interest. 
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision that
will have a foreseeable and naterial financial effect, different
from the effect on the general. public, on the official 
or on the
official's 
i'nrnediate farni.ly or on the following:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.
***
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the reg'ular course of business on
terns available to the public without regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or prornised to the public official
within 12 nonths prior to the tirne when the
decision is nade.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
enployee, or holds any position of managenent.
Section 87103 (a) , (c) , and (d) .
Comnissioner Caruso is a public official. 
(section 82048.)
Therefore, he is disqualified fron participating in a decision
that will have a foreseeable and naterial financial effect on any
of his economic interests, includingr a client l^rho has paid or
pronised to pay hin or his architectural consulting business $250
-or rno.e within- 12 nonths before the decision. (sections 87100 and
87103 (c) . )


Our F■■e No. ■-90-427
Page 4
Section 82041 defines trlocal government agencyri as a county,
city, or district 
of any kind including "any department, division,
bureau, office, board, commission or other agency of the
foregoingT. rr cenerally, the Coumission has treated different city
depaitnents as different agencies. r'or example, a ptanningl
courmission is a distinct agency fron the city council or the
department of city planning. (Stout Advice Letter, No. I-88-313,
copy enclosed. )
An official 
is disqualified from participating in a decision
irhich lrilI have a reasonably foreseeable and material financial
effect on a financial interest of the official. 
Disqual ification
Deans that the official 
cannot participate in discussions, vote on
a matter, or otherwise try to influence menbers of his or her own
agency. (Section 8710Oi Regutation 18700.1; Levinqer Advice
L;tte;, N6. I-88-328; West Advice Letter, No. A-88-413, copies
enclosed. )
Prohibited rrattempts to influence a decisiontr are described
in Regrulation 18700.1 (below) and include contacting any member,
offic6r, employee of the official-'s 
agency or any agency under the
appointive or budqetarv control, of the official's 
agency3
(a) with regard to a governmental decision
which is within or before an official's 
agency or
an alrency appointed by or subject to the budgetary
contiol ot tris or her agency, the official 
is
attenpting to use his or her official 
position to
influence the decision if, for the Purpose of
influencing the decision, the official 
contacts, or
appears beiore, or otherwise attempts to influence,
any meDber, officer, employee or consultant of the
agency. Attempts to influence include, but are not
finitea to, appearances or contacts by the official
on behalf of a business entity, client, or
custoner.
Regulation 18700.1.
Pursuant to subdivision (a), Commissioner caruso nay not
appear before his own agency (the planning conmission).or contact
tle departrnent of city planning staff (including the director of
planning) represent a client regardinq a matter which is, or may
tone, before the planning commission. Appearing before the
planning courmission rrould be an irnproper attenpt to use his
ofticiat position to influence a decision. sinilarly,
conrnissioner caruso cannot represent a client before the planning
director rrhen the decision is one which is appealable to, and rnay
be reconsidered by, the planning commission. The prohibition
applies because in both these situations, the decision to be rnade
is "rrithin or beforeti the public official's 
aqency.
Nevertheless, because the director of ptanning and his or her
department of city planning are not under the appointive or
budgetary control of the ptanning commission, comrnissioner caruso


may appear before the director of plannj.ng to represent a client
provided both of the folLowing conditions are met: (1) the
decision is one which is appealable directly to the city council
and, (2) Comnissioner caruso does not purport to act on behalf of
the planning commission. Purportinq to represent the planning
cornmission includes using official 
stationery to communicate with
the planning director or any other menber, officer, 
emPloyee or
consultant of the city planning department.
The Act does not prohibit an employee of Commissioner
caruso,s archj,tectural consultingT business fron representing an
applicant at the city planning departnent, planning conmission,
and city councit levels. 
However, keep in nind that Commissioner
caruso is disqualified if the decision foreseeabl,y and naterially
would affect his architectural consulting business or a source of
income to hin of $250 or more or any other financial interest of
the conmissioner. (Huquet, Jr. Advice Letter No. I-87-245, copy
enclosed. )
I trust this Ietter has provided you with guidance regarding
the duties and responsibilities of Planning Commissioner caruso
under the Act. If you have any further questions regarding these
natters, please feel free to contact ne at (916) 322-5901.
Our Fi■e No. I-90-427
Page 5

Enclosures

counsel, Legal Division
